rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARJORIE MILLER COMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Reserve
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to review the status of the $250,000 reserve and unresolved tax issues reported by the administrator of the estate. The administrator states in her report filed on 04/21/2022 that an extension has been filed for the estate tax return. It should be noted, however, that the administrator advised the Court in her declaration filed on 12/23/2021 before the hearing on her petition for final distribution that "no Federal 706 Estate Tax filing was due or tax payment required or was there a State of California , ET-1, due or tax payment required." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Parties have not reached a settlement. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: [Same issues since 4/21/2021.] a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. [See CRC 7.103] b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. DE-121 Notice was not given 15 days prior to the hearing to the following individuals directly but was given 1 day prior to the 10/6/2021 hearing: Michelle Bajjalieh, Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN COUCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/01/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petition page 1 lines 22-23 alleges the will was admitted to Probate by order of this court on March 9, 2021. The correct date is May 5, 2021. 2) Please file the mandatory judicial council form DE-174 to allow or reject the creditor claim. Please file a withdrawal of the creditor claim and/or proof of satisfaction of the claim. 3) William Couch was appointed with $1,500,000.00 bond. Please explain how this is sufficient when the I&A alleges over $5,000,000.00 and there appears to be over $6,625,809.79 assets on hand. It appears an ex parte petition for increase in bond in accordance with Local Rule 14.11 B. needs to be filed. 4) Please explain the legal basis and cite probate code sections in support of Frances Sanders gift being distributed to William Couch instead of being distributed according to item 8 in Decedent's Will. Please explain whether a petition to determine persons entitled to distribution needs to be filed under Probate Code 11700. 5) Please explain the legal basis and cite probate code sections for Michael Russell's disclaimer resulting in the assets going to the residuary estate. It appears as a result of the disclaimer Michael Russell's issue are entitled to distribution, notice, and have not waived the account. Please explain all issue for Michael Russell, give them required 15 days' notice, and have them waive an account or it appears the petition must be amended to have an account. 6) Under costs please explain entry 2/9/2021 Steve Adari MacDonald consultation Re: eviction of licensee from unit for $297.50 and file a receipt. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate Section 850 Petition To Recover Funds Distributed From Trust To Beneficiaries Without First Satisfying Assignment Lien, And
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether the petition should be ordered off calendar for failure to give notice or follow required court rules regarding courtesy copies and proposed orders.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALEXANDER T. FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: Decedent died in 2016. Petitioner alleges that probate was not opened earlier because she had been to 3 lawyers previously who each, after a year, withdrew. Petitioner states that she does not know whether any issues exist with the delay in opening the administration and believes there may be some bank accounts that she is not aware of. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUZANNE E. TURLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/31/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Codicil must be submitted to the court just like you submitted the original will in accordance with Probate Code 8200 et seq. 2) Please explain how the codicil is self-proving. Petitioner needs to prove by admissible evidence that either the presumption doesn't apply or the presumption needs to be rebutted for the lost codicil in accordance with Probate Code 6124. 3) Please give 15 days notice to Hardy L. Thomas on DE-121. (Probate Code 1202). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/16/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
